dialogue.utterance: Dispatch to Unit 901, suspected overdose at a residence on Baker Street.
Hi, I'm Mark, an EMT with the rescue squad. What made you call 911 today?
We found the man in the backyard, he's not breathing and doesn't look awake. He collapsed earlier.
Can you open your eyes, sir? Do you hear me?
Pt is unresponsive, no response to verbal or physical stimuli.
Maneuvering the patient for airway positioning. Airway is initially secured with an open/position OPA.
Pt is apneic, not breathing, and requires immediate assisted ventilations.
Initially no breathing (respiration 0), now at 10 breaths per minute with BVM assistance.
No carotid, radial, or femoral pulse detected. Pt is in cardiac arrest.
Pulse is 0, no radial or femoral pulses present.
Initial respiration is 0, now 10 with BVM assistance.
No blood pressure detected, initial assessment was 0/0.
Oxygen saturation is initially unrecorded, now 87% with assisted breathing.
Initial EKG is unrecorded, now in asystole.
Pupils are 4mm and non-reactive to light.
Superficial abrasions and contusion on nose, cheek, and left forearm. Pt found lying on his back near the fence.
Let's start CPR immediately. Manual compressions first.
Compressions initiated at 100-120 per minute.
Glasgow Coma Scale is 3. Pt is A&O x 0, no response to eye, verbal, or motor stimuli.
AED is applied now. Analyzing rhythm.
OPA is in place for airway management.
Pt's skin is pale, dry, and has poor turgor.
Initial glucose is 0. Second measurement at 246.
Administering 15 LPM of oxygen via BVM with good chest rise.
Still unresponsive. Respirations 10, pulse 0, BP undetectable.
Placing the patient on 4-lead ECG monitoring now.
He was last seen alive about an hour ago. Found lying with a partial bottle of Gin nearby.
Pt has a history of opioid abuse, alcohol dependence, and intoxication.
Based on findings and caller report, this is an unwitnessed overdose and cardiac arrest.
Administering naloxone 2mg intravenously due to reported drug use.
Given the unwitnessed arrest and full arrest criteria, we are following the general - cardiac arrest protocol.
Starting an 18g IV in the right arm now for medication and fluid administration.
Administering a 650ml bolus of normal saline to improve perfusion.
First dose of epinephrine 1:10,000 via IVP now.
Switching to the autopulse CPR device now for controlled compressions.
Second dose of epinephrine 1:10,000 administered via IVP. Pt still in asystole.
Ready for transport to [COMPANY_NAME]. Hot transfer for ongoing care.
Pt is being extricated from the backyard near the fence where he was found.
Pt is secured on a stretcher with three safety straps for transport in a confined space.
Oxygen saturation is 87%, EKG still in asystole.
Re-positioning BVM, clearing airway of dried dark matter possibly vomit.
Attempting to place a King airway but met resistance. OPA remains in place due to good compliance.
Pt is still A&O x 0. Second dose of epinephrine given with no improvement.
Continuing CPR with BVM and ongoing EKG monitoring during transport.
Pt's BP remains 0/0 and Glucose at 246 with no improvement after medications.
Third dose of epinephrine 1:10,000 administered upon arrival to ED.
Found lying on his back in the backyard, with a partial bottle of gin nearby, likely due to ingestion.
Glucose remains at 246, no evidence of hypoglycemia.
Pt was reportedly last seen alive about an hour ago with no exact time noted.
No known drug allergies reported by responders.
Pt's skin turgor is poor, consistent with possible dehydration due to chronic alcohol use.
No complaints of pain reported. Pain is 0 based on patient condition and unresponsiveness.
Rechecking pulse, still undetectable. BP remains 0/0.
ED has been called, pre-arrival report submitted.
All patient data and vitals confirmed before entering transport.
All medications and interventions per incident report are on file.
Oxygen at 15 LPM continues with BVM.
IV remains patent with 650 mL bolus administered.
Stretcher and patient safely moved into transport unit.
ED nurse has accessed and confirmed receiving all patient data.
Arrival alert sent. ED is aware of patient condition.
Pt continues in asystole. Compressions ongoing.
Third dose of epi on record now. No changes in vital signs observed.
Vital signs remain 0 pulse, 0 BP, 87% O2, asystole on EKG.
No additional findings from prior assessment on ambulance arrival.
All documentation, procedures, and medications cross-checked.
Pt is ready for arrival. All transport procedures complete.
Arrival at [COMPANY_NAME] estimated in 5 minutes.
Administering IV flush, ensuring med compatibility. BVM stable.
Pt remains unresponsive. No return of spontaneous circulation noted.
Autopulse functioning per manual at 120 compressions per minute.
Continuing compressions and assisted ventilation on autopulse/AED.
Pt remains in asystole with no signs of response or pulse.
Pt is now fully secured to the onboard stretcher for ED transfer.
Ready to offload now. Hot transfer initiated.
Pt arriving at [COMPANY_NAME] for emergency room treatment.
No spontaneous breathing or pulse. Pt is in full cardiac arrest.
Full documentation, medications, and procedures prepared for handoff.
No changes in response or status. Pt is unresponsive and stable for offload.
Finalizing care for pt en route to ED. Continue compressions.
